evals.prompt:
Annotate the vault note at /tmp/lit-annotate-eval0/vault/20-Sources/papers/smith2023-qtaim.md using the reading notes below. Fill in the Key points skeleton and suggest Galaxy links. The vault is at /tmp/lit-annotate-eval0/vault.

Reading notes:
"Key method: QTAIM + NCI analysis on a set of 40 hydrogen-bonded dimers using CCSD(T)/aug-cc-pVTZ density. Main result: electron density at BCPs correlates linearly with binding energy (R²=0.94) across all systems. IQA decomposition shows exchange-correlation term dominates stabilisation for strong H-bonds. Limitations: gas-phase only, no solvent effects, dimer geometry only. Interesting: IQA-Vcl term switches sign for weak H-bonds."
evals.expected_output: Key points section filled with Methods (QTAIM+NCI, CCSD(T)/aug-cc-pVTZ, 40 dimers), Key result (BCP correlation R²=0.94, IQA-Vcl sign switch), Limitations (gas phase, no solvent), Open questions. Galaxy suggestions for qtaim-related concepts. Approval prompt shown before writing. File written after approval.

assertions.description: All four Key points bullets contain non-empty content
Methods bullet names QTAIM, NCI, and CCSD(T) — not generic description
Key result includes R²=0.94 or equivalent quantitative value from reading notes
Skill checks for existing content before writing; empty bullets filled, filled bullets untouched
Preview diff shown and user asked for confirmation before writing to file
New Galaxy suggestions added as <!--[[...]]-->  not as confirmed [[links]]

evals.prompt:
I've read the paper at /tmp/lit-annotate-eval1/vault/20-Sources/papers/jones2024-elf.md. Fill in only the empty key points — the Methods bullet already has my notes. Vault at /tmp/lit-annotate-eval1/vault.

My notes: "Main finding: ELF basins in aromatic rings show quantised electron counts matching Hückel rule. Limitation: only tested on PAHs, not heteroaromatics. Open question: does basin topology change with substituents?"
evals.expected_output: Only empty Key points bullets filled (Key result, Limitations, Open questions). Methods bullet left unchanged because user already wrote it. Approval gate shown.

assertions.description: Methods bullet content unchanged from what was in the fixture — skill did not overwrite
Key result, Limitations, and Open questions bullets contain new content
Skill shows preview and requests confirmation before modifying the file

evals.prompt: Annotate /tmp/lit-annotate-eval2/vault/20-Sources/papers/garcia2022-iqa.md using the PDF at /tmp/lit-annotate-eval2/garcia2022.pdf. Vault at /tmp/lit-annotate-eval2/vault. This is for the PhD-Ch1-AIM4ML project.
evals.expected_output: Key points filled from PDF content. Open questions framed relative to AIM4ML project context (read from memory). Galaxy suggestions based on PDF content not already in the note. If PyMuPDF unavailable: graceful fallback message, skill continues with abstract.

assertions.description: Either PDF content drives the annotation, or a clear fallback message explains PyMuPDF unavailable and skill continues
Open questions bullet references AIM4ML project context (ML for QCT, transferability, etc.) — not generic
Only the single note file is modified; no other vault files written
